items.comment: RFC 9147 says receiving implementations MUST NOT enforce the sending-side epoch limit. BoringSSL reconstructs and tracks epochs in uint16_t and rejects ACK RecordNumber epochs above UINT16_MAX, so the receiver cannot represent or accept the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch space.
items.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch：The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time keying material changes ... Implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to wrap.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/dtls_record.cc:71, ssl/dtls_record.cc:80, ssl/dtls_record.cc:122, ssl/dtls_record.cc:176, ssl/dtls_record.cc:316, ssl/dtls_record.cc:366, ssl/dtls_record.cc:372, ssl/dtls_method.cc:43, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：RFC 9147 says receiving implementations MUST NOT enforce the sending-side epoch limit. BoringSSL reconstructs and tracks epochs in uint16_t and rejects ACK RecordNumber epochs above UINT16_MAX, so the receiver cannot represent or accept the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch space.
items.test_check: 已编写并运行 focused_static_checks.py 验证相关源码模式；BoringSSL runtime runner 因本机缺少 cmake/ninja/go/ssl_test 构建产物而阻塞，详见 runtime_blocker.log。
items.decision_reason: RFC 9147 says receiving implementations MUST NOT enforce the sending-side epoch limit. BoringSSL reconstructs and tracks epochs in uint16_t and rejects ACK RecordNumber epochs above UINT16_MAX, so the receiver cannot represent or accept the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch space.

categories.category: 64-bit epoch support limited to 16-bit implementation state

items.category: 64-bit epoch support limited to 16-bit implementation state
items.comment: RFC 9147 describes the connection epoch as an 8-octet counter whose low two octets appear in DTLSPlaintext. BoringSSL serializes and stores DTLS epochs as uint16_t/DTLSRecordNumber epochs, so it implements the low-two-octet wire form but not the full 64-bit connection epoch model.
items.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch：The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time keying material changes ... Implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to wrap.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/dtls_record.cc:71, ssl/dtls_record.cc:80, ssl/dtls_record.cc:122, ssl/dtls_record.cc:176, ssl/dtls_record.cc:316, ssl/dtls_record.cc:366, ssl/dtls_record.cc:372, ssl/dtls_method.cc:43, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：RFC 9147 describes the connection epoch as an 8-octet counter whose low two octets appear in DTLSPlaintext. BoringSSL serializes and stores DTLS epochs as uint16_t/DTLSRecordNumber epochs, so it implements the low-two-octet wire form but not the full 64-bit connection epoch model.

items.decision_reason: RFC 9147 describes the connection epoch as an 8-octet counter whose low two octets appear in DTLSPlaintext. BoringSSL serializes and stores DTLS epochs as uint16_t/DTLSRecordNumber epochs, so it implements the low-two-octet wire form but not the full 64-bit connection epoch model.

items.category: 64-bit epoch support limited to 16-bit implementation state
items.comment: RFC 9147 reserves epochs 4 through 2^64-1 for rekeyed application traffic, with a sending safety limit of 2^48-1. BoringSSL's DTLS epoch state is uint16_t and next_epoch fails at 0xffff, so it covers only a prefix of the specified application epoch space.
items.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch：The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time keying material changes ... Implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to wrap.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/dtls_record.cc:71, ssl/dtls_record.cc:80, ssl/dtls_record.cc:122, ssl/dtls_record.cc:176, ssl/dtls_record.cc:316, ssl/dtls_record.cc:366, ssl/dtls_record.cc:372, ssl/dtls_method.cc:43, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：RFC 9147 reserves epochs 4 through 2^64-1 for rekeyed application traffic, with a sending safety limit of 2^48-1. BoringSSL's DTLS epoch state is uint16_t and next_epoch fails at 0xffff, so it covers only a prefix of the specified application epoch space.
items.test_check: 已编写并运行 focused_static_checks.py 验证相关源码模式；BoringSSL runtime runner 因本机缺少 cmake/ninja/go/ssl_test 构建产物而阻塞，详见 runtime_blocker.log。
items.decision_reason: RFC 9147 reserves epochs 4 through 2^64-1 for rekeyed application traffic, with a sending safety limit of 2^48-1. BoringSSL's DTLS epoch state is uint16_t and next_epoch fails at 0xffff, so it covers only a prefix of the specified application epoch space.

categories.category: KeyUpdate epoch-limit response aborts instead of ignoring update_requested

items.category: KeyUpdate epoch-limit response aborts instead of ignoring update_requested
items.comment: BoringSSL avoids sending beyond its epoch limit, but the failure path is a too-many-key-updates error from next_epoch rather than ignoring update_requested while continuing the connection as RFC 9147 specifies near the sending limit.
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 8, Key Updates：KeyUpdates MUST be acknowledged ... implementations MUST NOT send records with the new keys or send a new KeyUpdate until the previous KeyUpdate has been acknowledged ... sending implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to exceed 2^48-1.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/tls13_both.cc:678, ssl/tls13_both.cc:701, ssl/d1_pkt.cc:122, ssl/d1_pkt.cc:130, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：BoringSSL avoids sending beyond its epoch limit, but the failure path is a too-many-key-updates error from next_epoch rather than ignoring update_requested while continuing the connection as RFC 9147 specifies near the sending limit.
items.test_check: 已编写并运行 focused_static_checks.py 验证相关源码模式；BoringSSL runtime runner 因本机缺少 cmake/ninja/go/ssl_test 构建产物而阻塞，详见 runtime_blocker.log。
items.decision_reason: BoringSSL avoids sending beyond its epoch limit, but the failure path is a too-many-key-updates error from next_epoch rather than ignoring update_requested while continuing the connection as RFC 9147 specifies near the sending limit.

items.comment: BoringSSL fragments handshake messages to fit the current MTU, but repeated retransmission only doubles the timer and resends the same flight. I did not find logic that backs off to a smaller record size when PMTU is unknown after repeated non-response.
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.4, PMTU Issues：If repeated retransmissions do not result in a response, and the PMTU is unknown, subsequent retransmissions SHOULD back off to a smaller record size, fragmenting the handshake message as appropriate.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/d1_both.cc:747, ssl/d1_both.cc:781, ssl/d1_both.cc:875, ssl/d1_both.cc:1031, ssl/d1_both.cc:1043。该路径显示：BoringSSL fragments handshake messages to fit the current MTU, but repeated retransmission only doubles the timer and resends the same flight. I did not find logic that backs off to a smaller record size when PMTU is unknown after repeated non-response.
items.test_check: 已编写并运行 focused_static_checks.py 验证相关源码模式；BoringSSL runtime runner 因本机缺少 cmake/ninja/go/ssl_test 构建产物而阻塞，详见 runtime_blocker.log。
items.decision_reason: BoringSSL fragments handshake messages to fit the current MTU, but repeated retransmission only doubles the timer and resends the same flight. I did not find logic that backs off to a smaller record size when PMTU is unknown after repeated non-response.

categories.category: RecordNumber model narrower than RFC structure

items.category: RecordNumber model narrower than RFC structure
items.comment: RFC 9147 expands RecordNumber to uint64 epoch plus uint64 sequence_number for ACK and AEAD inputs. BoringSSL encodes ACK fields as u64, but the source values come from DTLSRecordNumber with a 16-bit epoch and 48-bit sequence.
items.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch：The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time keying material changes ... Implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to wrap.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/dtls_record.cc:71, ssl/dtls_record.cc:80, ssl/dtls_record.cc:122, ssl/dtls_record.cc:176, ssl/dtls_record.cc:316, ssl/dtls_record.cc:366, ssl/dtls_record.cc:372, ssl/dtls_method.cc:43, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：RFC 9147 expands RecordNumber to uint64 epoch plus uint64 sequence_number for ACK and AEAD inputs. BoringSSL encodes ACK fields as u64, but the source values come from DTLSRecordNumber with a 16-bit epoch and 48-bit sequence.
items.test_check: 已编写并运行 focused_static_checks.py 验证相关源码模式；BoringSSL runtime runner 因本机缺少 cmake/ninja/go/ssl_test 构建产物而阻塞，详见 runtime_blocker.log。
items.decision_reason: RFC 9147 expands RecordNumber to uint64 epoch plus uint64 sequence_number for ACK and AEAD inputs. BoringSSL encodes ACK fields as u64, but the source values come from DTLSRecordNumber with a 16-bit epoch and 48-bit sequence.

items.comment: BoringSSL does not allow epoch wrap, but the wrap/too-many-key-updates condition is tied to 0xffff rather than the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch with a 2^48-1 sending limit. The no-wrap property is implemented, but at a much lower boundary.
items.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch：The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time keying material changes ... Implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to wrap.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/dtls_record.cc:71, ssl/dtls_record.cc:80, ssl/dtls_record.cc:122, ssl/dtls_record.cc:176, ssl/dtls_record.cc:316, ssl/dtls_record.cc:366, ssl/dtls_record.cc:372, ssl/dtls_method.cc:43, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：BoringSSL does not allow epoch wrap, but the wrap/too-many-key-updates condition is tied to 0xffff rather than the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch with a 2^48-1 sending limit. The no-wrap property is implemented, but at a much lower boundary.

items.comment: The implementation prevents wrap but its guard is prev == 0xffff, not an implementation of the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch non-wrapping model.
items.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 4.2, Sequence Number and Epoch：The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time keying material changes ... Implementations MUST NOT allow the epoch to wrap.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/dtls_record.cc:71, ssl/dtls_record.cc:80, ssl/dtls_record.cc:122, ssl/dtls_record.cc:176, ssl/dtls_record.cc:316, ssl/dtls_record.cc:366, ssl/dtls_record.cc:372, ssl/dtls_method.cc:43, ssl/dtls_method.cc:59。该路径显示：The implementation prevents wrap but its guard is prev == 0xffff, not an implementation of the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch non-wrapping model.

items.decision_reason: The implementation prevents wrap but its guard is prev == 0xffff, not an implementation of the RFC 9147 64-bit epoch non-wrapping model.

categories.category: missing DTLS 1.3 server HRR cookie validation path

items.category: missing DTLS 1.3 server HRR cookie validation path
items.comment: The DTLS 1.3 client path stores a HelloRetryRequest cookie and adds it to the next ClientHello. The BoringSSL server path contains comments that it could request a cookie but does not implement a DTLS 1.3 HRR cookie issuance/verification path.
items.standard_check: 复核 RFC 9147 Section 5.1, Denial-of-Service Countermeasures：The server then verifies the cookie and proceeds with the handshake only if it is valid ... cookies are only valid for the existing handshake and cannot be stored for future handshakes.
items.code_check: 复核代码证据 ssl/tls13_client.cc:255, ssl/tls13_client.cc:277, ssl/extensions.cc:2673, ssl/tls13_server.cc:825, ssl/tls13_server.cc:968。该路径显示：The DTLS 1.3 client path stores a HelloRetryRequest cookie and adds it to the next ClientHello. The BoringSSL server path contains comments that it could request a cookie but does not implement a DTLS 1.3 HRR cookie issuance/verification path.
items.test_check: 已编写并运行 focused_static_checks.py 验证相关源码模式；BoringSSL runtime runner 因本机缺少 cmake/ninja/go/ssl_test 构建产物而阻塞，详见 runtime_blocker.log。
items.decision_reason: The DTLS 1.3 client path stores a HelloRetryRequest cookie and adds it to the next ClientHello. The BoringSSL server path contains comments that it could request a cookie but does not implement a DTLS 1.3 HRR cookie issuance/verification path.
